description: Using .unwrap()/.expect() on fallible results in library code, turning recoverable errors into process-aborting panics.
body: Failure mode: library code unwraps an Option/Result that can legitimately be None/Err (parse, IO, lock), so a caller's valid-but-edge input panics and may abort the whole process or poison a lock. Detection signals: production panic with 'called Option::unwrap() on a None value'; clippy unwrap_used lint; crashes on untrusted input. Do not unwrap fallible values in reusable library paths. Safer fallback: return Result with a typed error (thiserror) or propagate with ?, reserve expect for true invariants with a message explaining why it cannot fail, and add a test feeding the error-producing input.
